description: Lightweight RAG corpus manifest for Puzzle V1. This is designed for plan A lexical evaluation and leaves room for plugging in a richer retriever later.

entries.title: Static puzzle scope and solver objective
entries.summary: The current solver only handles static puzzles, optimizes for minimum operation count, supports internal and edge goals, and shares legality semantics with the editor validation mode.

entries.title: UI preview versus solver boundary
entries.summary: Red and green preview boxes belong to UI presentation. The solver should reuse legality checks but should not own rendering or DOM behavior.

entries.title: Core entities, cell tags and zone types
entries.summary: The rule pack defines free, blocked, horizontal, vertical, target-lane and block-lane cell tags, plus target, block and fixed pieces, and spawn and goal zones.

entries.title: Goal modes full versus partial
entries.summary: Goal evaluation supports full and partial modes. Full requires the target footprint to be completely inside the goal. Partial only requires some overlap. This applies to both internal and edge goals.

entries.title: Basic movement legality
entries.summary: A move changes one piece in one orthogonal direction, cannot overlap other pieces, cannot enter blocked cells, and fixed pieces cannot move.

entries.title: Large piece footprint rules
entries.summary: Pieces larger than 1x1 move as a whole footprint. Occupancy and collision checks must validate the entire footprint, not just a single anchor cell. Large pieces cannot be split or rotated.

entries.title: Lane rules and movement priority
entries.summary: Horizontal and vertical are normal movement restriction tags. Target-lane and block-lane are role-specific lane constraints. When tags overlap, the configured priority decides which restriction dominates.

entries.title: Edge goal admission rules
entries.summary: Edge goals are special off-board legal areas only for matching target pieces. Non-target blockers cannot enter edge goals. The final step into a matching edge goal may relax normal lane restrictions.

entries.title: Shared legality interfaces
entries.summary: The architecture recommends shared legality interfaces: canOccupyFootprint, canMovePiece, evaluateGoals and validatePuzzle so that UI, Node and Java stay aligned.

entries.title: Validation versus solving
entries.summary: Structural validation checks whether a puzzle is well-formed, such as having target pieces and goals. Solving answers whether a valid puzzle is actually solvable and what the optimal steps are. These must not be confused.

entries.title: Search model and minimum operations objective
entries.summary: The baseline static solver uses BFS over puzzle states and legal moves, deduplicates serialized states, and optimizes for minimum operation count rather than shortest geometric distance.

entries.title: Node bridge and Java solver architecture
entries.summary: The recommended path keeps the browser editor in the frontend, uses Node as the local bridge, and uses Java as the formal solver engine. The semantics should stay aligned across layers.

entries.title: TC-01 multiple legal stops
entries.summary: One target piece on a 1x5 board with a goal in the middle. Used to verify that the solver can enumerate multiple legal stopping positions in one direction.

entries.title: TC-02 fixed adjacent blocks
entries.summary: Regression case for fixed blockers and collision checks. Used to verify that fixed pieces adjacent to movers correctly block the path.

entries.title: TC-03 stop mid opens path
entries.summary: Regression case where an intermediate stop is necessary because stopping earlier changes downstream reachability.

entries.title: TC-03 target lane priority
entries.summary: Regression case for target-lane priority. Used to verify how target-lane interacts with other movement restrictions.

entries.title: TC-04 large piece footprint
entries.summary: A 2x2 target piece with nearby fixed obstruction. Used to verify that occupancy and collision checks validate the entire large-piece footprint.

entries.title: TC-05 horizontal forbids vertical
entries.summary: Regression case for horizontal lane restrictions. Used to verify that horizontal-constrained movement forbids vertical movement.

entries.title: TC-06 edge goal full
entries.summary: Edge goal case for full mode. Used to verify that full edge goals require the entire target footprint to satisfy the goal.

entries.title: TC-06 edge goal partial
entries.summary: Edge goal case for partial mode. Used to verify that a large target can satisfy the goal with partial overlap when goalMode is partial.

entries.title: TC-07 block cannot enter edge goal
entries.summary: Regression case proving that a normal block piece cannot enter an edge goal area reserved for target pieces.

entries.title: TC-07 edge goal last step only
entries.summary: Regression case for final-step-only edge goal relaxation. Used to verify that normal lane restrictions are only relaxed on the final step into the edge goal.

entries.title: TC-08 shortest operation count
entries.summary: Simple shortest-path regression case. Used to verify that the baseline search objective is minimum operation count.
